name: xCell
description: Cell type enrichment analysis from gene expression data for 64 immune and stromal cell types.
long_description: xCell is a gene signatures-based method for inferring 64 immune and stromal cell types from gene expression data. It applies a novel technique for reducing associations between closely related cell types, producing a more accurate portrayal of the tissue cellular heterogeneity landscape. Used by over 20,000 researchers worldwide.

name: xCell 2.0
description: Next-generation robust algorithm for cell type proportion estimation. Predicts response to immune checkpoint blockade.
long_description: xCell 2.0 is a major update that provides robust cell type proportion estimation from bulk RNA-seq data. It features improved accuracy, new reference profiles, and the ability to predict response to immune checkpoint blockade treatments based on the tumor microenvironment cellular composition.

name: SingleR
description: Reference-based single-cell RNA-seq cell type annotation using pure transcriptomic data.
long_description: SingleR is an automatic annotation method for single-cell RNA-seq data. It leverages reference transcriptomic datasets of pure cell types to independently infer the cell type identity of each cell in a given single-cell dataset. Installed over 10,000 times from Bioconductor since September 2019.

name: CellMentor
description: Cell-type aware dimensionality reduction for single-cell RNA-sequencing data.
long_description: CellMentor provides cell-type aware dimensionality reduction that preserves biological structure in single-cell data, enabling better visualization and downstream analysis.

name: SLAYER
description: Computational framework for identifying synthetic lethal interactions through integrated analysis of cancer dependencies.
long_description: SLAYER is a computational framework that identifies synthetic lethal interactions by integrating multiple cancer dependency datasets, providing insights for targeted cancer therapy development.
